page: Microsoft Azure D32s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz and supports hyper-threading, providing two threads per core. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware flags such as AVX, AVX-512, and AES cryptography. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data places the D32s_v4 in the average performance tier for most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1477.49, and the multi-core score is 29831.05. Memory bandwidth tests show 1662.6 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 108.56 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Notably, the PassMark memory score of 2845.98 is rated as strong, placing it in the top 25%. In language model inference tests, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 260.56 tokens/sec using a small 135M model, though performance drops to a weak rating of 5.64 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a large 70B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D32s_v4 offers a balanced resource density of compute and memory without the added cost of bundled local storage or specialized GPU hardware. This makes it a standard option for workloads that rely on external Premium Storage. The server is suited for general-purpose workloads, including database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale text or image processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 32 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server features 16 physical cores, a 2.6 GHz clock speed, and a 35 MB L3 cache. It does not include local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results show average performance across most compute and memory metrics, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 29831.05 and a strong PassMark memory score of 2845.98. The server demonstrates strong text generation speeds of 260.56 tokens/sec on small language models, but exhibits weak performance on larger 70B models. This resource profile is suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, software compilation, and static web serving where local storage is not required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor, suited for database operations and general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, suited for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring thirty-two virtual cores and one hundred twenty-eight gigabytes of memory for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor
128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
No local storage or GPU, with support for Premium Storage
Strong PassMark memory performance and small-model text generation
Suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation
